segments.text: Welcome to our lecture on fault tolerance and consensus. In this lecture, we will discuss how distributed systems can handle failures and achieve consistency across nodes.
As we discussed earlier, network delays can affect NTP synchronization. This means that even with multiple servers, the accuracy of the time can be limited.
In distributed systems, detecting faults and achieving consensus across nodes can be challenging. We need to ensure that the system continues to function even when some nodes fail.
In a distributed system, we want to ensure that operations are executed in a linear order. However, with variable network delays, it's possible for nonlinearizable behavior to occur.
